title: feat: harden Z-Image deployment on Vast
author: ElliotEtag
pr_body_excerpt:
- Adds guarded Vast setup with production heartbeat disabled by default
- Pins the verified RTX 5090 stack and works around cuDNN VAE/SPAN crashes while keeping GPU execution
- Adds deterministic tunnel checks, bounded benchmarks, persistent caches, and process supervision
- Documents a shared Cloudflare Tunnel hostname across two Vast replicas

Live checks:
- Both RTX 5090 hosts pass 512x512, 1024x1024, and 768x1152 generation
- Host 1: 332/332 in 5m, 1.095 images/s, p95 3.713s
- Host 2: 69/69 in 60s, 1.090 images/s, p95 3.748s
- Shared tunnel has two healthy replicas; unauthenticated generation returns 403
- Both hosts match direct and tunneled pixels at all three sizes (RMS 0.000)
- Production traffic is reaching both Vast replicas with no observed 5xx/runtime errors

Static checks:
- `bash -n image.pollinations.ai/z-image/setup-vast.sh image.pollinations.ai/z-image/verify-vast.sh`
- `python3 -m py_compile image.pollinations.ai/z-image/server.py image.pollinations.ai/z-image/benchmark-vast.py`
- `git diff --check`

gist.headline: Two hives, one tunnel
gist.blurb: Z-Image now has two well-tended RTX 5090 nests behind one shared tunnel. Polly checked every pixel twice; the pixels, unusually, agreed.
gist.summary: Hardened Z-Image deployment on two Vast RTX 5090 replicas with guarded setup, deterministic Cloudflare Tunnel verification, persistent caches, process supervision, and GPU-safe workarounds for cuDNN VAE/SPAN crashes.
gist.impact: Z-Image generation now runs reliably across two healthy replicas at about 1.09 images per second with p95 latency below 3.75 seconds, while direct and tunneled outputs match exactly at 512×512, 1024×1024, and 768×1152.
gist.visual_concept: Depict Z-Image as a cozy dual-server garden: two chunky RTX 5090-powered retro terminals in separate little garden sheds connect through one glowing tunnel vine, each producing identical framed pixel landscapes. Polly, wearing a tiny site-reliability tool belt, compares matching image prints while tending code-flowers; the monitor robot can enthusiastically watch both healthy connections.
gist.image_prompt: Cozy pixel art scene of Polly, the cute yellow-and-dark-brown striped bee mascot with black antennae, oval black eyes, bright pink rectangular cheek blushes, a happy smile, and small rounded translucent light-blue wings, wearing a tiny tool belt while comparing two identical pixel landscape prints from two chunky retro GPU terminals in garden sheds. A glowing lime green (#ecf874) vine-like tunnel links the terminals, code-plants and soft pastel flowers grow around their persistent cache boxes, and a cute CRT-monitor robot watches both screens with happy square eyes; subtle pixel-font text reads “2 REPLICAS”. Cozy 8-bit pixel art aesthetic, soft pastel gradients, warm lighting, retro gaming vibes, warm cream and lavender background, dark purple (#110518) outlines, nostalgic but beautiful.
